result:
Hash table stores key-value pairs in array indexed by hash(key) % size. Collision when two keys hash to same slot.

Two main strategies:

Separate chaining. Each slot holds linked list (or tree). On collision, append to list. Lookup walks list comparing keys. Java HashMap uses this — switches to red-black tree when chain exceeds 8 entries.

Open addressing. Keep one entry per slot. On collision, probe for next free slot.
- Linear probing: try slot+1, slot+2, ... Cache-friendly but clusters.
- Quadratic probing: try slot+1, slot+4, slot+9, ... Breaks clusters.
- Double hashing: step size from second hash function. Best distribution.
Python dict, Go map use open addressing variants.

Deletion in open addressing needs tombstones — can't just empty slot or lookups break.

Load factor (entries/slots) drives resize. Chaining tolerates >1.0; open addressing resizes around 0.7 before probe chains explode.

Good hash function matters more than collision strategy. Bad hash = everything in one slot regardless.
